results.query: Does VoltCare cover accidental damage if I drop my phone?
results.rewritten_query: VoltCare accidental damage cover phone drop physical damage claim
results.suggested_response: Yes, VoltCare Plus and Complete both cover accidental damage to phones, including drops. Complete has no excess fee and no limit on claims, while Plus may have an excess. I can check which plan you have and explain the specific terms.
results.notes: Clear policy answer — Plus and Complete cover AD, Essential does not

results.query: How do I make a VoltCare claim?
results.rewritten_query: VoltCare warranty claim process procedure steps
results.suggested_response: You can make a VoltCare claim in four ways: online at voltex.co.uk/voltcare-claim (fastest, available 24/7), by phone on 0333 400 7000 option 2, in any Voltex store, or through the Voltex app if your account was created after March 2023. You'll need your VoltCare plan reference number starting with VC-.
results.notes: Straightforward claim process question

results.query: I bought my VoltCare plan in 2023 — do I still have to pay the excess on accidental damage?
results.rewritten_query: VoltCare plan 2023 accidental damage excess fee policy
results.suggested_response: Great news! Since you bought your VoltCare plan in 2023, it operates under our previous terms (Version 4.5) where no excess applies to accidental damage claims on the Plus tier. If you're ever asked to pay an excess, just ask us to check your plan version and we'll correct any error immediately.
results.notes: Version history question — pre-Oct 2024 plans have no excess. Agent must check CRM.

results.query: My OLED TV has burn-in — is that covered under VoltCare Complete?
results.rewritten_query: OLED TV burn-in damage VoltCare Complete warranty coverage claim
results.suggested_response: I'm sorry, but OLED burn-in isn't covered under any VoltCare plan, including Complete. This is because burn-in is a known characteristic of OLED display technology rather than a product fault. All manufacturers exclude this from their warranties too. I'd recommend using your TV's built-in pixel refresh feature regularly to help prevent this.
results.notes: OLED burn-in is explicitly excluded — common customer misconception

results.query: I have VoltCare on my washing machine and it broke down — how long will the repair take and what if they can't fix it?
results.rewritten_query: VoltCare washing machine breakdown repair time replacement policy
results.suggested_response: I can see you have VoltCare on your washing machine. If we can't fix it within 48 hours of receiving it, we'll replace it with no arguments - that's our guarantee. Let me arrange for our repair team to collect your machine and get this sorted for you quickly.
results.notes: Multi-part question covering white goods SLA and replacement trigger

results.query: My neighbour repaired my laptop before I knew about VoltCare — will the claim still be valid?
results.rewritten_query: VoltCare warranty claim validity third party repair before coverage
results.suggested_response: I need to check the specific terms around third-party repairs for you. Let me get a specialist to review your policy details and confirm whether your VoltCare coverage is still valid after the neighbour's repair work.
results.escalation_reason: Knowledge base lacks specific policy details about third-party repairs done before customer awareness of VoltCare coverage - requires specialist review
results.notes: Unauthorised repair voids cover — but agent cannot confirm without physical inspection

results.query: I bought a laptop two weeks ago and it has stopped working — what are my options?
results.rewritten_query: laptop hardware failure warranty claim repair options consumer rights
results.suggested_response: I'm sorry to hear your laptop has stopped working. Since you bought it just two weeks ago, you have several options including a full refund, exchange, or repair under your statutory rights. You may also have manufacturer warranty coverage. Let me check what VoltCare coverage you have and help you choose the best option.
results.notes: Within 30-day window — statutory right to full refund applies

results.query: I changed my mind about a TV I bought online three days ago — can I return it?
results.rewritten_query: TV return refund online purchase change of mind Consumer Rights Act cancellation period
results.suggested_response: Yes, you can return your TV within 30 days for a change of mind. Since it's a large TV, we'll arrange collection - it can't be posted back. There's a £4.99 fee deducted from your refund. I'll help you start the return process now.
results.notes: Online purchase within 14-day cancellation right under Consumer Contracts Regulations

results.query: My TV arrived damaged — I only noticed after the delivery team left. What happens now?
results.rewritten_query: TV damaged on arrival after delivery team left damaged goods return policy
results.suggested_response: I'm sorry your TV arrived damaged. Since you've just received it, this falls under our faulty goods policy. We'll arrange a return and replacement or full refund for you right away. Let me get this sorted - can you tell me your order number?
results.notes: Damage discovered post-delivery — 48-hour window applies

results.query: I dropped my laptop and cracked the screen — can I return it for a refund?
results.rewritten_query: laptop accidental damage cracked screen return refund policy
results.suggested_response: I understand that's frustrating. Unfortunately, accidental damage like dropping and cracking the screen isn't covered for returns - it's not considered a product fault. However, we can arrange a repair for you. Do you have VoltCare protection? If so, this could be covered depending on your plan level.
results.notes: AD is not a return — redirect to VoltCare AD claim or paid repair

results.query: I bought a TV 8 months ago and the screen has developed coloured lines — is Voltex responsible?
results.rewritten_query: TV screen defect coloured lines 8 months warranty coverage responsibility
results.suggested_response: Screen lines without any impact are typically a manufacturing or hardware fault, which would be covered. Since you're within your statutory rights period, we can arrange for an engineer to assess the TV and determine the best resolution for you.
results.notes: After 6 months burden shifts to customer — but screen fault at 8 months is likely a defect

results.query: The product listing said this laptop has a dedicated GPU but it only has integrated graphics — I want a full refund
results.rewritten_query: laptop product specification mismatch dedicated GPU integrated graphics full refund Consumer Rights Act
results.suggested_response: I understand your concern about the GPU specification mismatch. If our product description incorrectly listed a dedicated GPU when the laptop only has integrated graphics, you're entitled to a full refund under your statutory rights. Let me escalate this to ensure we resolve this properly for you.
results.escalation_reason: Product not as described situation requiring Team Leader escalation and Trading Standards team flagging as per policy
results.notes: Misdescription — statutory right at any time, must escalate to Trading Standards team

results.query: What is the difference between OLED and QLED televisions?
results.rewritten_query: OLED vs QLED television display technology comparison specifications
results.suggested_response: OLED pixels produce their own light and can switch off completely, giving perfect blacks and great contrast - ideal for dark rooms and movies. QLED uses enhanced LED backlighting with quantum dots for brighter, more colorful images - better for bright rooms and daytime viewing. OLED is generally more expensive but offers superior picture quality in darker environments.
results.notes: Direct FAQ match — highest similarity expected

results.query: How much RAM do I need in a laptop for everyday use?
results.rewritten_query: laptop RAM memory requirements everyday computing buying advice specifications
results.suggested_response: For everyday use like web browsing, email, and video calls, I'd recommend 16GB RAM. While 8GB is the minimum for Windows 11, it can feel sluggish with multiple browser tabs open. 16GB handles everyday multitasking comfortably and is our recommended standard for new purchases.
results.notes: Direct FAQ match

results.query: I want to use my new TV for gaming on PlayStation 5 — what should I look for?
results.rewritten_query: TV gaming PlayStation 5 specifications features requirements
results.suggested_response: For PlayStation 5 gaming, you'll need a TV with HDMI 2.1 port for 4K 120fps gaming, plus VRR (Variable Refresh Rate) to reduce screen tearing. ALLM automatically switches to Game Mode when your PS5 is detected. I'd recommend LG OLED for near-zero input lag, Samsung QN90D/QN95D for bright rooms, or Sony Bravia 9 for excellent PS5 integration.
results.notes: Specific gaming TV requirements — HDMI 2.1 is the key spec

results.query: Is a Chromebook suitable for a university student studying engineering?
results.rewritten_query: Chromebook specifications engineering student university compatibility software requirements
results.suggested_response: A Chromebook wouldn't be suitable for engineering studies. Engineering requires specialist software like CAD and 3D modelling tools that need Windows, plus a dedicated graphics card and 16GB+ RAM. Chromebooks are great for basic tasks but can't run the professional engineering software you'll need.
results.notes: Chromebook not suitable for engineering software — important to get right

results.query: What washing machine drum size do I need for a family of four?
results.rewritten_query: washing machine drum capacity size family household appliance buying advice
results.suggested_response: For a family of four, I'd recommend looking at our 10kg washing machines. These are specifically designed for 3-4 people or families who prefer doing fewer, larger washes. This size will handle your family's laundry efficiently without needing multiple smaller loads throughout the week.
results.notes: Straightforward buying guide question

results.query: My laptop battery drains really fast — is that covered under warranty?
results.rewritten_query: laptop battery defect fast drain warranty coverage claim
results.suggested_response: Fast battery drain could be covered, but it depends on the cause. If it's a manufacturing defect, it would be covered under your statutory warranty rights. However, VoltCare doesn't cover laptop batteries that have degraded naturally after heavy use. I'd need to check your laptop's age and usage to advise properly.
results.notes: Battery degradation vs premature failure — nuanced answer needed

results.query: My washing machine was supposed to be delivered today but it hasn't arrived
results.rewritten_query: washing machine delivery missed failed delivery white goods large appliance
results.suggested_response: I'm sorry your washing machine delivery didn't arrive as expected today. Let me check what happened with your order and get this resolved for you. I can see the delivery status and book a new delivery slot if needed.
results.notes: Missed large item delivery — standard process

results.query: Can I cancel my order before it is delivered?
results.rewritten_query: order cancellation before delivery pre-dispatch cancellation policy
results.suggested_response: Yes, you can cancel your order free of charge as long as it hasn't been dispatched yet. You can cancel online at voltex.co.uk/my-orders while the status shows 'Processing' or 'Preparing for despatch', or call us on 0333 400 7000 option 1. Your refund will be processed within 3 business days.
results.notes: Cancellation before despatch is straightforward

results.query: I need to change my delivery address — the order was placed an hour ago
results.rewritten_query: delivery address change order amendment recent purchase
results.suggested_response: I can help change your delivery address since your order was placed recently. Address changes need to be done by phone and can't be made online. Let me process this for you now, though please note it may affect your delivery date if the new address is in a different delivery zone.
results.notes: Address change requires phone call and updating two systems

results.query: I want Voltex to install my new washing machine when they deliver it
results.rewritten_query: VoltInstall washing machine installation delivery service
results.suggested_response: I can add VoltInstall washing machine installation for £49 to your order. This includes disconnecting your old machine, connecting the new one to existing plumbing, and running a test cycle. I'll need to add this before your delivery date - shall I arrange this for you?
results.notes: VoltInstall must be added 48 hours before delivery

results.query: I live in the Scottish Highlands — can I get my fridge-freezer delivered and will there be extra charges?
results.rewritten_query: Scottish Highlands delivery fridge-freezer large appliance delivery charges remote area surcharge
results.suggested_response: Yes, we can deliver your fridge-freezer to the Scottish Highlands! There's a £9.99 surcharge for large item delivery to cover the additional transport costs. This charge is clearly shown at checkout before you complete your order.
results.notes: Postcode restriction with surcharge — specific detail required

results.query: The delivery team said they couldn't get my fridge through the door — what happens now?
results.rewritten_query: large appliance delivery access failed installation fridge door width VoltInstall
results.suggested_response: I'm sorry the delivery couldn't be completed. Our standard fridges need doorways of at least 60cm wide to pass through. We'll need to arrange a return and refund, or you could consider a slimline alternative if available. Let me check your options and get this sorted for you.
results.escalation_reason: Failed delivery requires return processing and potential refund arrangement which may need Team Leader authorisation
results.notes: Access failure — failed delivery charge applies, but refund available for access issues

results.query: I am going to France next month — will I be charged extra to use my phone?
results.rewritten_query: EU roaming charges VoltMobile phone usage abroad France
results.suggested_response: Great news! If you're on a 60GB plan or higher, EU roaming is included at no extra charge - you can use your UK allowances in France as normal. If you're on a smaller plan, there may be charges. Can you tell me which VoltMobile plan you're currently on so I can give you the exact details?
results.notes: EU roaming — depends on plan tier

results.query: How do I bring my existing phone number to VoltMobile?
results.rewritten_query: number porting existing phone number transfer VoltMobile SIM
results.suggested_response: To bring your existing number to VoltMobile, you'll need to get a PAC code from your current network first. They must provide this within 2 hours when you request it. Once you have your PAC, give it to us and we'll handle the transfer. Your number may be unavailable for up to 24 hours during the process, which is normal.
results.notes: Number porting process — clear PAC code instructions

results.query: I have been charged for roaming but I never left the UK
results.rewritten_query: domestic roaming charges UK VoltMobile billing incorrect network fees
results.suggested_response: I understand your concern about these roaming charges. Let me check your account to see exactly when and where these charges occurred. Sometimes phones can connect to foreign networks near borders even when you haven't physically left the UK. I'll investigate this for you right away.
results.notes: Border proximity roaming — escalate to TL for goodwill credit assessment

results.query: My phone has been stolen — what should I do right now?
results.rewritten_query: phone stolen theft insurance claim replacement device VoltCare
results.suggested_response: I'm sorry to hear your phone was stolen. VoltCare doesn't cover theft, but if you have a VoltMobile plan with us, you may have device insurance included. You should also report the theft to police and contact your network provider to block the SIM. Would you like me to check if you have VoltMobile device insurance?
results.notes: Lost/stolen — bar SIM immediately, advise police report for insurance

results.query: I want to cancel my 24-month phone plan — I have 14 months left and I also owe money on the handset
results.rewritten_query: VoltMobile contract early termination cancellation outstanding handset balance 24 month plan
results.suggested_response: I can help you cancel your plan. You'll need to give 30 days notice and pay two amounts: an early termination charge for the remaining service (14 months × monthly service cost × 0.97 discount), plus your full outstanding handset balance. You can check your handset balance in the VoltMobile app under My Agreement.
results.notes: Must quote BOTH service ETC and handset settlement — critical agent error if only one quoted

results.query: There is a charge on my bill for a third-party service I did not sign up for
results.rewritten_query: third-party service billing charge unauthorized subscription VoltMobile
results.suggested_response: I can see the third-party charge on your bill. Let me help you resolve this. First, could you or anyone else using your phone have consented to this service? I can identify the service provider from your bill and guide you on next steps, including applying a charge bar if needed.
results.notes: Third-party charge — apply bar immediately, raise dispute, possible goodwill credit
